metadata.name: Docker Postgres Restore
metadata.description: One-shot Bash restore script for importing a .sql or .sql.gz PostgreSQL dump into a running Docker container with preflight checks, confirmation flow, optional drop/recreate, and dry-run support.
metadata.guide:
## Overview
This template renders a standalone `main.sh` restore script for PostgreSQL containers.

## What It Does
- Validates Docker, the target container, archive format, and required client binaries before doing anything destructive.
- Restores a plain SQL or gzipped SQL archive into either one database or an all-databases style dump.
- Supports an interactive confirmation step, `--yes` automation, `--dry-run`, and optional drop/recreate for single-database restores.

## Prerequisites
- The target PostgreSQL container must already be running.
- The archive should be a dump generated by `pg_dump`, `pg_dumpall`, or a compatible plain-SQL workflow.
- Export the password in the configured host environment variable before running when password auth is required.

## Run
1. Render the template and review the default container, archive, database, and safety values.
2. Export the password env var if needed, for example `export PGPASSWORD='...'`.
3. Run `bash main.sh --help` first, then execute the restore with either prompts enabled or `--yes` for automation.

## Notes
- `--drop-recreate` is only valid for a single target database, not for `all` database dumps.
- `--dry-run` prints the actions that would run without modifying the target.

## Docs
- [pg_dump Documentation](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/app-pgdump.html)
- [psql Documentation](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/app-psql.html)

items.name: container_name
items.title: Container Name
items.description: Name of the running PostgreSQL container.

items.name: archive_path
items.title: Archive Path
items.description: Absolute path to the .sql or .sql.gz dump archive on the Docker host.

items.name: postgres_user
items.title: Postgres User
items.description: Database user used by psql for the restore.

items.name: postgres_database
items.title: Database Name
items.description: Target database to restore, or 'all' for an all-databases dump.

items.name: postgres_password_env
items.title: Password Env Variable
items.description: Host environment variable that stores the PostgreSQL password.

items.name: drop_recreate_database
items.title: Drop/Recreate Database
items.description: If enabled, terminate connections and recreate the target database before restore.

items.name: dry_run
items.title: Dry Run
items.description: Print planned actions without changing the container.
